checks.assertion: A committed domain transaction creates exactly one River job through direct PostgreSQL.
A rolled-back domain transaction creates no River job through direct PostgreSQL.
Queue, priority, max attempts, and scheduled state/availability are preserved through direct PostgreSQL.
A committed domain transaction creates exactly one River job through PgBouncer transaction mode.
A rolled-back domain transaction creates no River job through PgBouncer transaction mode.
Queue, priority, max attempts, and scheduled state/availability are preserved through PgBouncer transaction mode.
riverqueue 0.7.0 unique insertion is incompatible with the River 0.40.0 migration-006 index shape.
checks.cause: The client targets ON CONFLICT(kind, unique_key), while migration 006 removes the qualifying index and introduces the unique_states design.

checks.assertion: A Go River 0.40.0 worker inserts, claims, executes, retries, cancels, schedules, and finalizes through direct PostgreSQL.
A Go River 0.40.0 worker inserts, claims, executes, retries, schedules, and finalizes through transaction-mode PgBouncer with PollOnly.
Neither cross-client nor same-client JobCancel propagated to an already-running worker context with riverpgxv5 0.40.0 and PollOnly.
checks.cause: PollOnly suppresses the listener, while the local no-listener control helper returns early for the listener-capable riverpgxv5 driver.
checks.required_resolution: Provision direct PostgreSQL queue control, separately validate a session-mode endpoint, add and verify a separate cancellation control plane, or reopen the broker decision.

checks.assertion: River 0.39 migrates and works on schema 6; after River 0.40 upgrades to schema 7, each version inserts the v1 contract and the other version consumes it.
River 0.40 Go workers consume committed Python fixtures through direct and transaction-mode PollOnly profiles.
Python and River 0.39 strict decoders validate the deterministic Go v1 fixture.
A direct-mode worker killed by SIGKILL is rescued and completes as attempt 2 with one recorded first-attempt error.
A PollOnly worker killed by SIGKILL is rescued and completes as attempt 2 with one recorded first-attempt error.
Every claim/insert/mark crash window converges to one River job and one delivered outbox row.

At a 250 ms fetch interval, the 20-sample PollOnly queue-start p95 stays within the 600 ms compatibility limit; backend and pool connection deltas remain within six with zero cancelled acquires. Exact measurements are in the generated harness result.
